metadata.title: The State of Peer Review in Empirical Software Engineering: A Community Survey on Review Load, Quality, and GenAI Use
metadata.description:
Artifact repository accompanying a questionnaire survey (n=120) published in ACM SEN about the state of peer review in empirical software engineering (ESE). It contains:

  • the full questionnaire as a PDF (questionnaire.pdf)
  • the raw data of the 120 responses (raw-questionnaire-responses.csv)
  • the cleaned, transformed, and labeled data used for analysis together with plots (results.xlsx)

data.title: The State of Peer Review in Empirical Software Engineering: A Community Survey on Review Load, Quality, and GenAI Use

metadata.title: Asimov: A framework for coordinating parameter estimation workflows
metadata.description: Asimov is a general-purpose workflow orchestration platform for automating complex scientific analyses on high-throughput computing clusters. It provides pipeline-agnostic architecture, automated job management, and version-controlled configuration for reproducible computational workflows. Battle-tested in production for gravitational wave parameter estimation by LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA collaborations, with extensible design supporting diverse scientific domains.
metadata.notes: If you use Asimov in your research, please cite the JOSS paper (preferred-citation below). For specific software versions, also cite the Zenodo release DOI.

data.title: Asimov: A framework for coordinating parameter estimation workflows

metadata.title: Linguistic Variation in Pakistani and British Crime Press Reportage: A Multidimensional Analysis
metadata.description: The present study investigates linguistic variation in the crime press reportage of Pakistani and British print media using Douglas Biber's (1988) Multidimensional (MD) analytical framework. The central research question examines the extent to which Pakistani crime press coverage differs linguistically from British crime press coverage across Biber's five textual dimensions: Dimension 1 (Involved vs. Informative), Dimension 2 (Narrative vs. Non-Narrative), Dimension 3 (Explicit vs. Situation-Dependent), Dimension 4 (Overt vs. Covert Expression), and Dimension 5 (Abstract vs. Non-Abstract). A balanced corpus of 1,000 texts was compiled from five leading Pakistani and five leading British newspapers published between September and November 2017, yielding a total of 324,086 words. Texts were tagged and scored using Biber's 1988 tagger software, and mean dimension scores were calculated for each corpus. The findings reveal that Pakistani crime press reportage is significantly more informative (D1: -21.74), less narrative (D2: +1.98), more explicit (D3: +5.97), more covert (D4: -3.09), and more abstract (D5: +4.31) than British crime press coverage (D1: -13.18; D2: +3.25; D3: +2.99; D4: -2.61; D5: +2.52). These differences are attributed to cross-cultural divergences, the post-colonial linguistic context of Pakistani English, and differing readership demands. The study establishes Pakistani English crime press reporting as a distinct sub-register within the broader framework of World Englishes.

journal.title: Linguistic Forum - A Journal of Linguistics

data.title: Linguistic Variation in Pakistani and British Crime Press Reportage: A Multidimensional Analysis

metadata.title: Fault-Tolerance Scaffold Simulator: Hardware Errors, Measurement Errors, and Software Decoding
metadata.description: A classical repetition-code simulator that introduces the systems logic of fault tolerance through hardware errors, measurement errors, and majority-vote software decoding. Version 2.0 adds a multi-cycle error-correction model, a three-panel interactive figure covering majority-vote curves, operating region heatmap, and resource estimation, and a companion guided exercises document with pre- and post-exercise questions and answers.
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it as below.

data.title: Fault-Tolerance Scaffold Simulator: Hardware Errors, Measurement Errors, and Software Decoding

metadata.title: SBAS-InSAR Time Series Modeling with Coherence-Driven Hotspot Analysis for Detecting Land Deformation in the Mississippi River Delta
metadata.description: This dataset contains Sentinel-1 SBAS-InSAR processed results for land deformation analysis across the Mississippi Delta region (2020–2024). The data include MintPy-derived products such as line-of-sight (LOS) velocity maps, temporal coherence files, and deformation time-series outputs, along with coherence-based hotspot and network quality plots. These datasets were generated using ASF HyP3 InSAR products and processed entirely with the open-source MintPy framework. The collection supports research on deltaic land subsidence, deformation hotspot detection, and coastal resilience assessment.
keywords: Sentinel-1, SBAS, InSAR, MintPy, Mississippi Delta, Land Deformation, Coherence, Subsidence, Remote Sensing, Geophysics

dates.description: Data collected and processed for SBAS-InSAR time series analysis (2020–2024)

references: European Space Agency (ESA). (2020–2024). Sentinel-1 SAR data. Distributed by Alaska Satellite Facility (ASF) VERTEX, https://search.asf.alaska.edu
U.S. Geological Survey (USGS). (2024). 3D Elevation Program (1/3 arc-second DEM). The National Map, https://apps.nationalmap.gov/
Multi-Resolution Land Characteristics (MRLC) Consortium. (2021). National Land Cover Database (NLCD) 2021. U.S. Geological Survey, https://www.mrlc.gov/

Yunjun, Z., Fattahi, H., & Amelung, F. (2019). Small baseline InSAR time series analysis: MintPy software for SAR data processing. Geoscientific Model Development, 12, 3075–3096. https://doi.org/10.5194/gmd-12-3075-2019
Alaska Satellite Facility (ASF). (2024). HyP3 On-Demand InSAR Processing Platform, https://hyp3.asf.alaska.edu/

data.title: SBAS-InSAR Time Series Modeling with Coherence-Driven Hotspot Analysis for Detecting Land Deformation in the Mississippi River Delta

metadata.title: Archivos base para la modelación hidráulica en HEC-RAS del río Molino, Popayán, Cauca
metadata.description:
Este conjunto de archivos corresponde a los insumos base empleados para el desarrollo de un ejercicio académico de modelación hidráulica 1D en HEC-RAS, aplicado a un tramo urbano del río Molino en la ciudad de Popayán, Cauca.

Los archivos se organizan en carpetas temáticas que permiten estructurar el proceso de construcción del modelo hidráulico. La carpeta 01_shp contiene el shapefile de coberturas utilizado como insumo para la definición espacial de condiciones del modelo. La carpeta 02_raster incluye la ortofotografía y el modelo digital de elevación empleados para la preparación del terreno y la interpretación del tramo de estudio. La carpeta 03_prj contiene el archivo de proyección asociado al sistema de coordenadas MAGNA Colombia Oeste. La carpeta 04_caudales incluye la serie de tiempo de caudales máximos utilizada como referencia hidrológica para el ejercicio. Finalmente, la carpeta 05_hec se deja vacía como espacio sugerido para almacenar los archivos generados durante el proceso de modelación en HEC-RAS.

Este material se comparte con fines estrictamente académicos y busca apoyar el aprendizaje práctico de la modelación hidráulica 1D, incluyendo la preparación del terreno, incorporación de ortofotografía, definición de rugosidades, generación de secciones transversales, configuración de condiciones de flujo permanente y análisis de resultados hidráulicos.

Los archivos pueden ser utilizados como material complementario del video tutorial asociado, orientado a la construcción de un modelo hidráulico 1D en HEC-RAS para la evaluación de profundidades, velocidades de flujo y áreas inundables en un entorno urbano.

El modelo hidráulico asociado al ejercicio se desarrolló mediante HEC-RAS, software del Hydrologic Engineering Center del U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, empleado para análisis hidráulico en una y dos dimensiones, incluyendo cálculos de flujo permanente, flujo no permanente, transporte de sedimentos y calidad del agua.
references: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Hydrologic Engineering Center. (2026). HEC-RAS River Analysis System, User's Manual. Hydrologic Engineering Center
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Hydrologic Engineering Center. (2026). HEC-RAS River Analysis System [Computer software]. Hydrologic Engineering Center

data.title: Archivos base para la modelación hidráulica en HEC-RAS del río Molino, Popayán, Cauca

metadata.title: Achieving residential energy targets in the EU: A multi country comparison of regulatory and economic instruments (code and data)
metadata.description:
First public release of the code and data for: Achieving residential energy targets in the EU: A multi country comparison of regulatory and economic instruments. Ece Özer, Francesca Conselvan, Daniel Harringer, Andreas Müller, Lukas Kranzl. Energy Policy (under review), 2026.

Contents

  • data/ — Invert/Opt building stock model outputs (final and primary energy demand, area-specific PED, gross floor area) for four policy scenarios under constant and decreasing PEF assumptions, plus model inputs (primary energy factors, MEPS threshold distributions, worst-performing-buildings data) for seven heating dominated EU Member States.
  • scripts/ — Python scripts reproducing the paper's figures (FED carrier mix, sPED reduction trajectories, four-driver decomposition, target-achievement heatmaps, 2020 baseline).
  • figures/ — the figures as used in the paper.

Code is released under MIT; data under CC BY 4.0.

Produced within the EPBD.wise project (EU Grant Agreement No. 101120194).

creators.name: Özer, Ece
creators.affiliation: Energy System Economics Lab, Institute of Net-Zero Energy Systems, TU Wien

creators.name: Kranzl, Lukas
creators.affiliation: Energy System Economics Lab, Institute of Net-Zero Energy Systems, TU Wien

metadata.notes: If you use this software or data, please cite the associated paper.

data.title: Achieving residential energy targets in the EU: A multi country comparison of regulatory and economic instruments (code and data)
